info.name: Order Service
info.description: CRUD requests for order-service. Create Order is ASYNCHRONOUS: it publishes an OrderCreatedEvent to Kafka and returns immediately with status CREATED — it does NOT reserve stock or charge payment inline. Run 'Get Order By Id' a moment after creating to see it settle into CONFIRMED or CANCELLED once inventory-service/payment-service/Kafka have processed the event. As of Phase 4: a CONFIRMED order also gets a Shipment record (see GET /api/orders/{id}/... note below — there is no shipment endpoint yet, check inventory-service/payment-service logs or kafka-ui's shipment.created topic to see it). Set base_url to the gateway (http://localhost:8080) if you want to go through the API Gateway instead of hitting the service directly.

item.name: 1. Create Order (success path - use a productId with enough stock)

request.description: Publishes OrderCreatedEvent and returns immediately with status CREATED. Note the response 'id' field and use it (or set {{order_id}}) for the 'Get Order By Id' request below to watch it become CONFIRMED.

item.name: 2. Create Order (failure path - request more than available stock)

request.description: Requests far more stock than exists. Response is still 201 with status CREATED immediately. A moment later, inventory-service's Kafka listener fails to reserve stock, publishes inventory.failed, and order-service's listener marks this order CANCELLED — poll 'Get Order By Id' to observe it.

item.name: 3. Get Order By Id (poll this after Create Order to see CREATED -> CONFIRMED/CANCELLED)

request.description: CONFIRMED means: payment succeeded, a Shipment row was created, and ShipmentCreatedEvent was published to shipment.created (check kafka-ui, port 8090, to see it — no consumer/endpoint reads it yet). CANCELLED from inventory failure means nothing was ever reserved (no compensating events). CANCELLED from payment failure additionally publishes OrderCancelledEvent to order.cancelled, which inventory-service consumes to release the stock it reserved, and payment-service consumes to check for a refund.
